title: Post-Employment Medical Examination. When the seafarer suffers work-
content: 29 related injury or illness occurring between the date of commencing duty and the date upon 30 which they are deemed duly repatriated, or arising from their employment between those 31 dates and still requires medical attention upon repatriation, the seafarer must, within three 32 (3) working days upon arrival at the point of hire, undergo a post-employment medical 33 examination and treatment by a company-designated physician until the seafarer is 34 declared fit to work or assessed disability grading. 35 36 The cost of the post-employment medical examination with the company-designated 37 physician shall be borne by the principal/employer. 38 39 40 Recruitment Outside Registered Office 41 42
